steps.title: Identify the crucial comparison
steps.content: The passage presents two ships with three decks: the Dreadnought (not first-rate) and the Caledonia (first-rate). That two ships can have identical deck counts yet different rankings is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the comparison to the choices
steps.content: Choice A states that not all three-deck ships were first-rate — a conclusion proven by the Dreadnought and Caledonia, which have identical decks but different rankings. The other choices either contradict the passage or speculate beyond it. That matches choice A — A is correct.
